name: Lyell Immunopharma, Inc.
business: Lyell Immunopharma is a late-stage clinical cell therapy company developing next-generation autologous CAR T-cell product candidates for patients with cancer, focusing on hematologic malignancies and solid tumors.
sellsTo: patients with relapsed or refractory large B-cell lymphoma
patients with advanced metastatic colorectal cancer

exposures.sentence: Our lead product candidate, rondecabtagene autoleucel (ronde-cel, also known as LYL314), is a dual-targeting CD19/CD20 CAR T-cell therapy designed to increase complete response rates and prolong the duration of response as compared to the approved CD19 targeted CAR T-cell therapies.

catalysts.event: Initiation of PiNACLE-H2H Phase 3 trial first patient dosing
catalysts.sentence: Patient dosing has commenced in the PiNACLE-H2H Phase 3 trial, which randomizes patients to either ronde-cel or investigator's choice of axicabtagene ciloleucel (axi-cel) or lisocabtagene maraleucel (liso-cel).

catalysts.event: PiNACLE pivotal single-arm trial expected to enroll approximately 120 patients
catalysts.sentence: This trial is a seamless expansion of the 3L+ cohort in our ongoing Phase 1/2 clinical trial and is expected to enroll approximately 120 patients at approximately 25 sites.

catalysts.event: PiNACLE-H2H Phase 3 trial expected to randomize approximately 400 patients
catalysts.sentence: We expect to randomize approximately 400 patients 1:1 to either ronde-cel or investigator's choice of axi-cel or liso-cel.

catalysts.event: LYL273 Phase 1 clinical trial ongoing to establish recommended Phase 2 dose
catalysts.sentence: LYL273 is currently undergoing evaluation in an ongoing Phase 1 trial to establish the recommended Phase 2 dose.

tamClaims.claim: The approved CD19 CAR T-cell therapies for NHL are expected to achieve worldwide sales in 2026 of approximately four billion dollars.
tamClaims.sentence: The approved CD19 CAR T-cell therapies for NHL are expected to achieve worldwide sales in 2026 of approximately four billion dollars.

tamClaims.claim: The worldwide net sales for currently approved CRC products is expected to reach 12 billion dollars by 2032.
tamClaims.sentence: The worldwide net sales for currently approved CRC products is expected to reach 12 billion dollars by 2032.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 30,000 patients in the U.S. with LBCL, 40% to 50% are refractory to, or relapse following, first-line treatment and we estimate approximately 12,000 to 15,000 patients in the United States with LBCL progress to 2L treatment.
tamClaims.sentence: Of the approximately 30,000 patients in the U.S. with LBCL, 40% to 50% are refractory to, or relapse following, first-line treatment and we estimate approximately 12,000 to 15,000 patients in the United States with LBCL progress to 2L treatment.
